item.title: Three Charts Capturing Micron’s Record Rise to $1 Trillion
item.description: Micron Technology Inc.’s incredible rally this year has launched the memory-chip firm into an elite group of stocks worth at least $1 trillion, setting at least one record along the way.

item.title: The SpaceX IPO Is the Perfect Embodiment of Markets
item.description: Momentum trumps everything.

item.title: Mexico Cuts 2026 Growth Forecast as Investment Falters
item.description: Mexico’s central bank cut its estimate for 2026 growth to 1.1% from a previous 1.6%, as investment disappoints amid trade uncertainty and supply-chain disruptions.

item.title: Chile’s Atacama Desert Is Becoming Sweet Spot for Energy Storage
item.description: The Atacama desert in northern Chile is emerging as a magnet for investment in energy storage that allows vast solar plants to extend supply after sundown.

item.title: Meta to Sell AI Chatbot Subscriptions to Offset Spending
item.description: Meta Platforms Inc. is selling consumer subscriptions to its Meta AI chatbot for the first time, a key step toward building a business that would help offset hundreds of billions of dollars in artificial intelligence investments by the company.

item.title: Goldman Says Iran War Dollar Surge Weighed on Treasury Demand
item.description: A stronger dollar during the first month of the US-Iran conflict spurred foreign official institutions to sell Treasuries, according to Goldman Sachs.

item.title: NASA Chief Expects Permanent Base on Moon as Soon as Early 2030s
item.description: NASA Administrator Jared Isaacman said the agency will begin building a moon base with near-monthly robotic landings starting in 2027, with astronauts potentially living on the lunar surface for months at a time by the early 2030s.

item.title: Uber Buys Aspex’s Stake in Delivery Hero After Takeover Offer
item.description: Uber Technologies Inc. bought out Aspex Management’s shares of Delivery Hero SE, boosting its stake to 36.83% as the ride-hailing company negotiates a deal to buy the German food-delivery platform.

item.title: White House Reviews CFTC Plan to Supervise Prediction Markets
item.description: The White House is reviewing a new US Commodity Futures Trading Commission proposal that would lay out guidelines for event contracts, the agency’s latest move to mark its turf as it battles with states over who gets to regulate the exploding prediction markets.

item.title: Dutch Police Seize Servers, Deal Blow to Russian Hackers
item.description: The Dutch financial crimes agency raided two data centers in the country and seized 800 servers.

item.title: NY’s Budget Adds Second-Home Tax, Nixes Levy on Cash Purchases
item.description: New York lawmakers outlined a revenue package for the state budget that includes a new surcharge on pricey second homes in the city, but omits a proposal to tax all-cash property purchases.

item.title: Sony Takes Final Bow As Standalone TV Maker With New Bravia Sets
item.description: Sony Group Corp. unveiled two high-end televisions, which are likely to be the Japanese company’s last premium sets before its storied home theater business merges with TCL Electronics Holdings Ltd. next year.

item.title: Copper’s Giant Tariff Trade Is Back and Squeezing Global Market
item.description: Copper traders are once again scouring the world for metal to send to the US, as renewed speculation about import tariffs revives a trade that’s upended the $300 billion-a-year market.

item.title: Strait of Hormuz Traffic Fades to a Crawl After Supertankers Exit
item.description: Commercial shipping through the Strait of Hormuz dwindled to only a few mostly Iran-linked vessels crossing on Wednesday, underscoring the stop-start nature of traffic through the world’s most vital energy chokepoint.

item.title: New CLOs at Blackstone, Guggenheim Boast Key Perk: Less Software
item.description: Blackstone Inc. and Guggenheim Investments are among investment firms reducing software exposure in some of their recent collateralized loan obligation deals out of growing caution over AI disruption.

item.title: Portugal Central Bank Sounds Alarm on Surging Home Prices
item.description: Portugal’s housing market, the hottest in the euro area, poses the biggest financial risk to the country as soaring prices and rising household debt increase vulnerabilities, central bank Governor Alvaro Santos Pereira said Wednesday.

item.title: El Niño Threat Prompts Peru to Extend Halt on Anchovy Fishing
item.description: Peru has extended a halt on anchovy fishing due to a warming of the Pacific waters off the country’s coast, as the threat of a weather-roiling El Niño looms.

item.title: Popular X Account Sparks Massive Rally in Little-Known Chipmaker
item.description: Shares of a little-known European chip manufacturer soared more than 70% on Wednesday after being touted by a popular social media account.

item.title: Germany’s Gas Grids Urge Overhaul of Storage Filling Incentives
item.description: Germany’s gas grid operators are calling for sweeping changes to the way storage refills are encouraged, warning that stubbornly low inventory levels are heightening the risk of supply shortages ahead of winter.

item.title: Investors Say Time Is Now for Pemex to Tap Global Debt Markets
item.description: Investors say the time is right for state-owned Petroleos Mexicanos SA to sell global bonds for the first time in more than three years.

item.title: Spotify, Netflix Woo Podcaster Jay Shetty From YouTube in New Deal
item.description: The agreement is worth as much as $100 million over multiple years.

item.title: AI Coding Startup Cognition Raises $1 Billion at $26 Billion Value
item.description: Cognition AI Inc. has raised more than $1 billion in a new funding round at a $26 billion valuation, the latest sign of strong demand for companies using artificial intelligence for software development.

item.title: Copasa Shares Tumble After Bids for Stake Fall Below Minimum Price
item.description: The two bids presented for a 30% stake in the Brazilian water utility Copasa fell below the minimum price expected by the Minas Gerais state government, according to four people familiar with the matter.

item.title: US Eases Some Tariffs on Taiwan to Formalize Trade Agreement
item.description: The Trump administration is removing some tariffs on imports from Taiwan to implement parts of a previously agreed trade deal with the US.

item.title: Canada’s Carney to Meet Macron in Paris Ahead of June G7 Summit
item.description: Canadian Prime Minister Mark Carney will make an early visit to Paris ahead of the Group of Seven leaders summit next month to meet with French President Emmanuel Macron, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: Erdogan Seeks to Meet Trump Around Soccer World Cup to Discuss NATO
item.description: Turkish President Recep Tayyip Erdogan is trying to arrange a meeting with President Donald Trump around the time of a Turkey-US World Cup soccer game in Los Angeles next month, people familiar with the matter said.

item.title: DeSantis Proposes Axing Taxes on Primary Homes in Florida
item.description: Florida Governor Ron DeSantis unveiled a much-awaited proposal to gradually eliminate property taxes on the vast majority of primary homes in the state, a measure that would transform the way the state finances itself.

item.title: Oil Barges Forced to Carry Less Fuel as Rhine Water Level Drops
item.description: Falling water levels on one of Europe’s most important rivers are restricting the amount of oil barges can carry, adding more pressure to the region’s fuel supply chains as the Iran war grinds on.

item.title: India Issues Tender to Import Fertilizer Ahead of Monsoon Sowing
item.description: India is seeking offers from global suppliers to buy 1.7 million tons of urea, a widely used nitrogen fertilizer, ahead of the monsoon sowing season, as the Middle East conflict disrupted gas supplies for domestic output.

item.title: US Lenders Posted Winning First Quarter, FDIC Says
item.description: Key metrics at US banks improved during the first quarter despite war and rising interest rates, even as the industry grappled with a modest increase in paper losses, the Federal Deposit Insurance Corp. reported.
